title: list + closed commands
description:
## Context
Part of the vima implementation. Plan: `PLAN-REFINED.md`, Phase 2, Step 2.4.
WISHLIST #6: consistent filters on all list commands. list and closed share the same pipeline but differ in implicit filters and sort order.

## Task
Wire two commands in `src/main.rs`:

### list
1. `Store::open()`, `read_all()`, `compute_reverse_fields()`
2. Convert FilterArgs to Filter via `Filter::from_args()`
3. No implicit status filter — user provides `--status` if desired
4. Apply filters: `filter.matches()` on each ticket
5. Sort: priority ASC, then id ASC
6. Apply limit if provided
7. Output via `output_many(tickets, pluck, count)`

### closed
1. Same load pipeline
2. Force status filter to Closed (regardless of user --status flag)
3. Sort by file mtime DESC via `std::fs::metadata(path)?.modified()?`
   - Need to read mtime from `.vima/tickets/{id}.md` for each closed ticket
   - mtime updates on any write, not just close — acceptable approximation
4. Default limit=20 if `--limit` not provided (applied in handler, not in FilterArgs)
5. Output via `output_many`

Both commands support all FilterArgs: --tag, --type, --priority, --assignee, --limit, --pluck, --count.

## Files
- `src/main.rs` — modify: implement list and closed command handlers

## Dependencies
- Depends on: vima-uky (reverse field computation) — needs compute_reverse_fields
- Depends on: vima-vj1 (start/close/reopen) — need closed tickets for closed command tests
- Depends on: vima-i9u (Filter module) — needs Filter::from_args, apply_filters
- Produces: list pipeline pattern reused by ready/blocked

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Integration test: create 3 tickets with different priorities, `vima list` returns all sorted by priority ASC
2. Integration test: `vima list --status open` filters to open only
3. Integration test: `vima list --tag backend` filters to tagged tickets
4. Integration test: `vima list --priority 0-1` returns only P0-P1
5. Integration test: `vima list --limit 1` returns exactly 1
6. Integration test: `vima list --pluck id` returns flat array of IDs
7. Integration test: `vima list --count` returns integer count
8. Integration test: close 2 tickets, `vima closed` returns them sorted by mtime DESC
9. Integration test: `vima closed` defaults to limit=20
10. `cargo build` and `cargo test` pass

title: ID generation + validation + resolution
description:
## Context
Part of the vima implementation. Plan: `PLAN-REFINED.md`, Phase 1, Step 1.4.
ID resolution is used everywhere a ticket ID is accepted — command args AND relationship targets.

## Task
Create `src/id.rs` with:

1. `validate_id(id: &str) -> Result<()>`:
   - Reject empty, path traversal (`/`, `\\`, `..`, `\0`), all-dots, leading dot
   - Allow only `[a-zA-Z0-9._-]`
   - Return `Error::InvalidField("id contains invalid characters")` on failure

2. `resolve_id(dir: &Path, input: &str, exact: bool) -> Result<String>`:
   - Trim input, call validate_id first
   - Exact mode: only check `{input}.md` exists, return Ok or NotFound
   - Fuzzy mode: try exact match first, then substring search across all `.md` files (exclude `.md.tmp`)
   - 0 matches → `Error::NotFound`, 1 match → Ok, 2+ matches → `Error::AmbiguousId`

3. `get_prefix(vima_root: &Path) -> Result<String>`:
   - Read `.vima/config.yml` for stored prefix
   - If config missing, compute from directory name: first letter of each `-`/`_` segment (e.g., `my-project` → `mp`), single segment → first 2 chars
   - Return the prefix string

4. `generate_id(prefix: &str, tickets_dir: &Path) -> Result<String>`:
   - Format: `{prefix}-{nanoid(4, alphanumeric)}`
   - nanoid alphabet: `0123456789abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz` (lowercase alphanumeric)
   - Check collision against existing files in tickets_dir
   - Retry up to 10 times on collision
   - If all 10 collide, return `Error::IdExists` suggesting `--id`

## Files
- `src/id.rs` — create: all ID functions
- `src/main.rs` — modify: add `mod id;`

## Dependencies
- Depends on: vima-ksx (Error types + JSON stderr output) — uses Error::NotFound, AmbiguousId, InvalidField, IdExists
- Note: does NOT depend on vima-ii2 (ticket data model) — id.rs works with filenames and error types only, not Ticket structs
- Produces: ID resolution used by every command that accepts a ticket ID

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Unit test: `validate_id("vm-a3x9")` succeeds
2. Unit test: `validate_id("../etc/passwd")` returns InvalidField
3. Unit test: `validate_id("")` returns InvalidField
4. Unit test: `validate_id(".hidden")` returns InvalidField
5. Unit test: `resolve_id` with exact=true only matches full ID
6. Unit test: `resolve_id` with exact=false matches substring, returns AmbiguousId for multiple matches
7. Unit test: `get_prefix` computes `"mp"` from dir name `"my-project"`, `"vi"` from `"vima"`
8. Unit test: `generate_id` produces format `{prefix}-{4 chars}`
9. `cargo build` succeeds, `cargo test` passes

title: Project scaffold + clap skeleton
description:
## Context
Part of the vima implementation — an AI-agent-first ticketing CLI in Rust.
Plan: `PLAN-REFINED.md`, Phase 1, Step 1.1.

## Task
1. Create `Cargo.toml` with these dependencies (exact versions from plan):
   - `clap = { version = "4.6", features = ["derive"] }`
   - `serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }`
   - `serde_json = "1.0"`
   - `gray_matter = "0.3"`
   - `colored = "3.1"`
   - `jiff = "0.2"`
   - `nanoid = "0.4"`
   - `which = "8.0"`
   - `exec = "0.3.1"`
   - Release profile: `strip = true, lto = true, codegen-units = 1`

2. Create `src/main.rs`:
   - Parse `Cli` struct via `clap::Parser`
   - Match on `Commands` enum, each variant prints `eprintln!("not implemented: <command>")` and calls `std::process::exit(1)`
   - Store the global `--pretty` and `--exact` flags on the `Cli` struct

3. Create `src/cli.rs` with ALL clap derive structs from the plan:
   - `Cli` struct with `#[command(name = "vima")]`, global `--pretty` and `--exact` (with `env = "VIMA_EXACT"`) flags
   - `Commands` enum with all variants: Create, Show, List, Ready, Blocked, Closed, Update, Start, Close, Reopen, IsReady, AddNote, Dep, Undep, Link, Unlink, Init, Help, External (external_subcommand)
   - `CreateArgs`: title (Option<String>), --type, --priority, --assignee, --estimate, --tags (comma-separated String), --description, --design, --acceptance, --dep (Vec<String>), --blocks (Vec<String>), --parent, --id, --batch
   - `UpdateArgs`: id (String), --title, --description, --design, --acceptance, --priority, --tags, --assignee, --estimate, --status (Option<Status>), --type (Option<TicketType>)
   - `ShowArgs`: id (String), --pluck
   - `FilterArgs`: --status (Option<Status>), --tag (-T, Vec<String>), --type (-t), --priority (-p, Option<String> for ranges), --assignee (-a), --limit, --pluck, --count
   - `IdArgs`: id (String) — shared by start, reopen, is-ready
   - `AddNoteArgs`: id (String), text (Option<String>)
   - `LinkArgs`: id_a (String), id_b (String) — used by both Link and Unlink
   - `UndepArgs`: id (String), dep_id (String)
   - `ClosedArgs`: filter (flatten FilterArgs) — default limit 20 applied in handler
   - `CloseArgs`: ids (Vec<String>), --reason (Option<String>)
   - `InitArgs`: --with_instructions (bool)
   - `DepArgs` with `DepCommands` subcommand: Add(AddDepArgs), Tree(TreeArgs), Cycle
   - `AddDepArgs`: id, dep_id, --blocks (bool)
   - `TreeArgs`: id, --full (bool)
   - Note: `Status` and `TicketType` enums are imported from `ticket.rs` (not yet created). For now, define placeholder enums in `cli.rs` with a `// TODO: import from ticket.rs` comment, OR use `String` types for status/type fields and mark with TODO. The next bead (ticket data model) will provide the real enums.

## Files
- `Cargo.toml` — create: full project manifest
- `src/main.rs` — create: entry point with clap dispatch stubs
- `src/cli.rs` — create: all clap derive structs

## Dependencies
- Depends on: nothing (first bead)
- Produces: compilable project skeleton that all other beads build on

## Acceptance Criteria
1. `cargo build` succeeds with no errors
2. `cargo run -- --help` shows vima help with all subcommands listed
3. `cargo run -- create "test"` prints "not implemented" to stderr and exits 1
4. `cargo run -- list` prints "not implemented" to stderr and exits 1
5. All subcommand stubs follow the same pattern
6. `cargo run -- --exact create "test"` parses without error (global flag works)

title: init command
description:
## Context
Part of the vima implementation. Plan: `PLAN-REFINED.md`, Phase 1, Step 1.7 (init portion).
init must work before any other command. It creates the .vima/ directory structure.

## Task
Wire the `init` command in `src/main.rs`:

1. Create `.vima/` directory in CWD (not via walk-up — init always targets CWD)
2. Create `.vima/tickets/` subdirectory
3. Compute ID prefix via `id::get_prefix()` from directory name
4. Store prefix in `.vima/config.yml` only if config doesn't already exist. Format:
   ```yaml
   prefix: mp
   ```
5. If `.vima/` already exists in CWD, skip directory creation (idempotent)
6. Do NOT overwrite existing `config.yml` (preserves prefix stability)
7. Stderr: `eprintln!("Initialized vima in .vima/")`
8. Note: `--with-instructions` flag is parsed but not handled yet (separate bead vima-u70)

9. **Add `Store::resolve_id` convenience method** to `src/store.rs`:
   ```rust
   impl Store {
       pub fn resolve_id(&self, input: &str, exact: bool) -> Result<String> {
           id::resolve_id(&self.tickets, input, exact)
       }
   }
   ```
   This is the first bead where both `store.rs` and `id.rs` exist. This convenience method is called by all command handlers that accept ticket IDs (create, show, update, close, dep add, etc.).

## Files
- `src/main.rs` — modify: implement init command handler
- `src/store.rs` — modify: add `resolve_id` convenience method (delegates to `id::resolve_id`)

## Dependencies
- Depends on: vima-1s0 (ID generation) — uses get_prefix() for prefix computation; id.rs must exist for Store::resolve_id
- Depends on: vima-gz3 (YAML writer) — needs Store with write capability for config
- Produces: .vima/ directory structure required by all other commands; Store::resolve_id used by every command accepting ticket IDs

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Integration test: `vima init` creates `.vima/` and `.vima/tickets/` in a temp dir
2. Integration test: `vima init` creates `.vima/config.yml` with computed prefix
3. Integration test: running `vima init` twice doesn't error and doesn't overwrite config.yml
4. Integration test: prefix computed correctly from dir name (e.g., `my-project` → `prefix: mp`)
5. stderr contains "Initialized vima in .vima/"
6. Unit test: `Store::resolve_id` delegates to `id::resolve_id` correctly
7. `cargo build` and `cargo test` pass

title: Batch create from stdin
description:
## Context
Part of the vima implementation. Plan: `PLAN-REFINED.md`, Phase 3.
WISHLIST #3: batch create with back-references. The highest-complexity feature. Turns N+M sequential commands into 1 for plan decomposition.

## Task
Create `src/batch.rs` with:

1. `batch_create(store: &Store, exact: bool) -> Result<Vec<Ticket>>`:
   - Read JSON lines from stdin via `stdin.lock().lines()`
   - Read at most 1001 lines (to detect overflow). If > 1000 → `Error::InvalidField("batch exceeds 1000 line limit")`
   - For each line:
     a. Check line length ≤ 1 MB → `Error::InvalidField("batch line N exceeds 1MB limit")`
     b. Parse as `serde_json::Value`
     c. Resolve back-references in "dep", "blocks", "parent" fields via `resolve_value_backrefs`
     d. Build ticket from spec: validate ID (validate_id), check collision against BOTH on-disk AND created_ids from earlier lines, run would_create_cycle for each dep
     e. Write ticket via store.write_ticket
     f. Handle "blocks": add this ticket's ID to each target's deps via store.add_dep (create add_dep method on Store if not yet present)
     g. Track created_ids for back-reference resolution

2. `resolve_backrefs(value: &str, created_ids: &[String]) -> Result<String>`:
   - If starts with `$`: parse index (1-based), look up in created_ids
   - `$1` → created_ids[0], `$2` → created_ids[1], etc.
   - Invalid index → `Error::InvalidBackref`
   - Non-$ values pass through unchanged

3. `resolve_value_backrefs(spec: &mut serde_json::Value, field: &str, created_ids: &[String]) -> Result<()>`:
   - If field is string: resolve single backref
   - If field is array: resolve each element

4. `create_from_spec(store: &Store, spec: &serde_json::Value, created_ids: &[String], exact: bool) -> Result<Ticket>`:
   - Extract title (required), type, priority, tags, assignee, estimate, description, design, acceptance, dep, parent, id
   - Generate ID if not provided
   - Validate all fields same as single create
   - Write ticket

5. Wire in `src/main.rs`: when CreateArgs.batch is true, call batch_create instead of single create. Output: JSON array of all created tickets.

**Partial failure:** NOT transactional. If line N fails, lines 1..N-1 are already on disk. Error message includes line number and list of already-created ticket IDs.

## Files
- `src/batch.rs` — create: batch_create, backreference resolution, create_from_spec
- `src/store.rs` — modify: add add_dep method if not present
- `src/main.rs` — modify: wire batch mode in create handler, add `mod batch;`

## Dependencies
- Depends on: vima-vj1 (start/close/reopen) — need close for testing dep resolution in batch
- Depends on: vima-f9c (dep add + undep) — need would_create_cycle for cycle detection in batch
- Produces: batch create — the final WISHLIST feature

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Integration test: batch create 2 tickets via stdin JSON lines → returns array of 2 tickets
2. Integration test: batch with `$1` back-reference in dep → second ticket deps contains first ticket's ID
3. Integration test: batch with `$1` in blocks → first ticket's deps updated to include second
4. Integration test: batch with `$1` in parent → parent field resolved
5. Integration test: batch with invalid `$99` → error with line number
6. Integration test: batch exceeding 1000 lines → error
7. Integration test: batch with cycle ($1 deps $2, $2 deps $1) → error on the cyclic line
8. Integration test: partial failure — first ticket created, second fails → first still on disk
9. Unit test: resolve_backrefs("$1", &["id-a"]) → "id-a"
10. Unit test: resolve_backrefs("$0", &[]) → error
11. `cargo build` and `cargo test` pass

title: ready + blocked + is-ready commands
description:
## Context
Part of the vima implementation. Plan: `PLAN-REFINED.md`, Phase 2, Steps 2.4-2.5.
WISHLIST #8: bidirectional deps in output. ready/blocked are the primary dep-aware queries.

## Task
Wire three commands in `src/main.rs`:

### ready
1. Load all tickets, compute reverse fields
2. Filter to open/in_progress status
3. Keep only tickets where ALL deps are closed (empty deps = ready)
4. Apply remaining filters from FilterArgs (tag, type, priority, assignee)
5. Sort by priority ASC, id ASC
6. Apply limit, output via output_many with pluck/count

### blocked
1. Load all tickets, compute reverse fields
2. Filter to open/in_progress status
3. Keep only tickets where ANY dep is NOT closed
4. For each blocked ticket, compute `open_deps: Vec<String>` — the dep IDs that are not closed
5. Add `open_deps` to the JSON output. Implementation: serialize ticket to serde_json::Value, then insert `"open_deps"` key before outputting. This avoids adding a non-persistent field to the Ticket struct.
6. Apply remaining filters, sort, limit, output

### is-ready
1. Resolve single ticket ID
2. Load all tickets
3. If ticket is closed → return `{"ready": true, "open_deps": []}` with exit 0
4. Collect deps with status != closed into open_deps
5. If open_deps empty → `{"ready": true, "open_deps": []}` exit 0
6. Else → `{"ready": false, "open_deps": ["vm-b7k2", ...]}` exit 2
7. Exit code 2 for blocked (not 1 — 1 is reserved for errors like NotFound)

## Files
- `src/main.rs` — modify: implement ready, blocked, is-ready command handlers

## Dependencies
- Depends on: vima-08i (list + closed) — shares the load-filter-sort-output pipeline
- Produces: ready/blocked/is-ready for workflow automation

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Integration test: create A (no deps) and B (deps=[A]), `vima ready` returns only A
2. Integration test: close A, `vima ready` now returns both A and B
3. Integration test: `vima blocked` returns B (before A is closed), with open_deps=["A"]
4. Integration test: `vima is-ready <B>` → exit 2, `{"ready": false, "open_deps": ["A"]}`
5. Integration test: close A, `vima is-ready <B>` → exit 0, `{"ready": true, "open_deps": []}`
6. Integration test: `vima is-ready <closed-ticket>` → exit 0, ready=true
7. Integration test: ready/blocked support --tag, --priority filters
8. Integration test: `vima ready --count` returns integer
9. `cargo build` and `cargo test` pass

title: dep tree command
description:
## Context
Part of the vima implementation. Plan: `PLAN-REFINED.md`, Phase 2, Step 2.9.
Renders dependency tree from a root ticket. Two modes: deduplicated (default) and full.

## Task

### In `src/deps.rs`:

1. Define `TreeNode` struct:
   ```rust
   #[derive(Serialize)]
   pub struct TreeNode {
       pub id: String,
       pub status: Status,
       pub title: String,
       pub deps: Vec<TreeNode>,  // recursive children in tree
   }
   ```

2. `build_dep_tree(tickets: &[Ticket], root_id: &str, full: bool) -> Result<TreeNode>`:
   - Resolve root ticket
   - **Cycle safety:** track visited nodes. If encountered again, create a leaf TreeNode with title suffixed ` [cycle]`. Do not recurse.
   - **Dangling deps:** if dep ID doesn't exist in tickets, create leaf TreeNode with title `<missing>` and ` [missing]` suffix
   - **Default mode (full=false):** deduplicate — each node appears once at its deepest position. Compute max_depth map via iterative DFS first, then build tree showing each node only at its max depth.
   - **Full mode (full=true):** no dedup, nodes may repeat (but cycle marker still applies)
   - Sort children by subtree depth ASC, then by ID

### In `src/main.rs`:

3. Wire `DepCommands::Tree(TreeArgs)`:
   - Load all tickets
   - Call build_dep_tree(tickets, id, full)
   - Output: JSON TreeNode (recursive structure)
   - Note: --pretty rendering of the tree is in a later bead

## Files
- `src/deps.rs` — modify: add TreeNode struct and build_dep_tree
- `src/main.rs` — modify: wire dep tree command

## Dependencies
- Depends on: vima-f9c (dep add + undep) — need dep add for creating test graphs
- Produces: dep tree for visualizing dependency chains

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Integration test: A depends on B depends on C → tree from A shows A→B→C nested structure
2. Integration test: diamond (A→B, A→C, B→D, C→D) in default mode → D appears once at deepest
3. Integration test: same diamond with --full → D appears twice
4. Integration test: dangling dep (dep references nonexistent ID) → [missing] marker in output
5. Integration test: cycle in data → [cycle] marker, no infinite loop
6. Unit test: children sorted by subtree depth ASC then ID
7. `cargo build` and `cargo test` pass

title: show command
description:
## Context
Part of the vima implementation. Plan: `PLAN-REFINED.md`, Phase 1, Step 1.7 (show portion).
Show displays a single ticket's full details including computed reverse fields.

## Task
Wire the `show` command in `src/main.rs`:

1. Resolve ID via `store.resolve_id(input, exact)` — supports partial matching unless --exact
2. Load ALL tickets via `store.read_all()`
3. Call `deps::compute_reverse_fields()` on all tickets to populate blocks/children
4. Find the target ticket in the computed list
5. Output via `output_one(ticket, pluck)` — supports --pluck on ShowArgs

## Files
- `src/main.rs` — modify: implement show command handler

## Dependencies
- Depends on: vima-nyp (create command) — need to create tickets to show them (for tests)
- Depends on: vima-uky (Reverse field computation, bead ID: vima-uky) — needs compute_reverse_fields for blocks/children
- Produces: show command used for inspecting tickets; pattern reused by other single-ticket commands

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Integration test: create a ticket, then `vima show <id>` returns full JSON with all fields
2. Integration test: `vima show <partial-id>` resolves to the correct ticket
3. Integration test: `vima show <partial-id> --exact` returns not_found if not exact match
4. Integration test: `vima show <id> --pluck title` returns bare string value
5. Integration test: `vima show <id> --pluck title,priority` returns object with two fields
6. Integration test: show includes computed `blocks` and `children` fields
7. Integration test: `vima show nonexistent` → exit 1, error="not_found"
8. `cargo build` and `cargo test` pass

title: add-note + link + unlink commands
description:
## Context
Part of the vima implementation. Plan: `PLAN-REFINED.md`, Phase 2, Step 2.7.
Parity features: add-note for timestamped annotations, link/unlink for symmetric relationships.

## Task

### add-note
Wire `add-note` command in `src/main.rs`:

1. Resolve ticket ID via `store.resolve_id(input, exact)`
2. Get note text:
   - If text argument provided, use it
   - Else read from stdin (up to 64 KB cap). Use `std::io::Read::take(65536)` to enforce limit.
   - If stdin is empty (EOF with no content) → `Error::InvalidField("note text is empty")`
3. Append `Note { timestamp: now_iso8601(), text }` to ticket.notes
4. Write ticket via `store.write_ticket()`
5. Reload with `store.load_and_compute(id)` to populate computed fields, then `output_one`
6. Stderr: `Added note to {id}`

### link
Wire `link` command:
1. Resolve both IDs (id_a, id_b from LinkArgs)
2. Verify both tickets exist BEFORE modifying either
3. Load both tickets
4. Add id_b to ticket_a.links (if not already present)
5. Add id_a to ticket_b.links (if not already present)
6. If both already linked → no-op, skip writes
7. Write both tickets
8. Reload both with `store.load_and_compute` for computed fields
9. Stdout: JSON array of both updated tickets
10. Stderr: `Linked {id_a} ↔ {id_b}`

### unlink
Wire `unlink` command:
1. Resolve both IDs, verify both exist
2. Load both tickets
3. Remove id_b from ticket_a.links, remove id_a from ticket_b.links
4. If neither link existed → no-op for consistency
5. Write both tickets
6. Stdout: JSON array of both updated tickets
7. Stderr: `Unlinked {id_a} ↔ {id_b}`

## Files
- `src/main.rs` — modify: implement add-note, link, unlink handlers

## Dependencies
- Depends on: vima-nyp (create command) — need tickets for testing
- Depends on: vima-uky (Reverse field computation) — uses `store.load_and_compute()` for output with computed fields
- Produces: add-note used for annotations; link/unlink for cross-references

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Integration test: `vima add-note <id> "My note"` → notes array contains entry with text and timestamp
2. Integration test: pipe text via stdin → `echo "stdin note" | vima add-note <id>` works
3. Integration test: `vima add-note <id>` with no text and no stdin → error
4. Integration test: `vima link A B` → A.links contains B, B.links contains A
5. Integration test: `vima link A B` twice → idempotent, no duplicates
6. Integration test: `vima unlink A B` → A.links no longer contains B, B.links no longer contains A
7. Integration test: link with nonexistent ticket → error before any modification
8. `cargo build` and `cargo test` pass

title: dep add + undep commands
description:
## Context
Part of the vima implementation. Plan: `PLAN-REFINED.md`, Phase 2, Step 2.6.
WISHLIST #7: --blocks syntax on dep. Cycle detection prevents invalid dependency graphs.

## Task

### dep add
Wire `dep add` subcommand in `src/main.rs`:

1. Parse AddDepArgs: id, dep_id, --blocks flag
2. Normal mode: `vima dep add A B` — A depends on B. Add B to A's deps list.
3. `--blocks` mode: `vima dep add A B --blocks` — A blocks B. Add A to B's deps list.
4. Resolve both IDs via store.resolve_id
5. Duplicate check: if dep already exists in target's deps list, return success (no-op)
6. Cycle detection BEFORE write:

Add to `src/deps.rs`:
```rust
pub fn would_create_cycle(tickets: &[Ticket], from: &str, to: &str) -> Option<Vec<String>>
```
- DFS from `to` following deps. If we reach `from`, there's a cycle.
- Track visited nodes. Use `path: HashMap<String, String>` to reconstruct cycle path.
- Use `entry().or_insert()` (not plain insert) to keep first-discovered parent.
- Return `Some(cycle_path)` if cycle found, None otherwise.

7. If cycle detected → return `Error::Cycle(path)` (exit code 2)
8. Add dep, write ticket, reload with load_and_compute, output_one
9. Stderr: `Added dep {dep_id} to {id}` (or reverse for --blocks)

### undep
Wire `undep` command:
1. Resolve both IDs
2. Load ticket
3. Remove dep_id from deps list. If not present → `Error::InvalidField("dep not found")`
4. Write, reload, output_one
5. Stderr: `Removed dep {dep_id} from {id}`

Also: go back and wire cycle detection into the create command's `--dep` handling (if not already done in the create bead).

## Files
- `src/deps.rs` — modify: add would_create_cycle, reconstruct_path
- `src/main.rs` — modify: implement dep add and undep handlers

## Dependencies
- Depends on: vima-uky (reverse field computation) — deps.rs already exists with compute_reverse_fields
- Depends on: vima-nyp (create command) — need tickets for testing; also wire cycle check into create --dep
- Produces: dep add with cycle detection used by batch create; undep for dep removal

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Integration test: `vima dep add A B` → A's deps contains B
2. Integration test: `vima dep add A B --blocks` → B's deps contains A
3. Integration test: `vima dep add A B` twice → no-op second time, no duplicate
4. Integration test: create A→B→C chain, `vima dep add C A` → exit 2, cycle error with path
5. Integration test: cycle error JSON on stderr contains `"cycle": [...]`
6. Integration test: `vima undep A B` → B removed from A's deps
7. Integration test: `vima undep A X` where X not in deps → exit 1, "dep not found"
8. Unit test: would_create_cycle with known cyclic graph returns Some(path)
9. Unit test: would_create_cycle with acyclic graph returns None
10. `cargo build` and `cargo test` pass

title: YAML template writer + atomic writes
description:
## Context
Part of the vima implementation. Plan: `PLAN-REFINED.md`, Phase 1, Step 1.5 (write half).
The template-based writer produces clean, deterministic, git-diffable YAML. No serde_yml — we control the output format exactly.

## Task
Add to `src/store.rs`:

1. `needs_quoting(s: &str) -> bool`:
   - Empty string → true
   - Leading/trailing whitespace (s != s.trim()) → true
   - Contains tab → true
   - Starts with YAML-special: `-`, `*`, `&`, `!`, `|`, `>`, `%`, `@`, backtick, `,`, `[`, `]`, `{`, `}`, `#`, `?`, `'`, `"` → true
   - Contains `:` or `#` or `\n` → true
   - Lowercase matches `true|false|null|yes|no|on|off` → true
   - Parses as f64 → true
   - Otherwise → false

2. `yaml_scalar(s: &str) -> String`:
   - If needs_quoting: escape `\` first, then `"`, `\n`, `\t`, `\r`. Wrap in double quotes.
   - Otherwise: return as-is

3. `write_yaml_array(out: &mut String, key: &str, items: &[String])`:
   - Write `key: [item1, item2]` with each item through `yaml_scalar`
   - Empty vec → `key: []`

4. `write_yaml_notes(out: &mut String, notes: &[Note])`:
   - If empty, write `notes: []`
   - Otherwise write YAML sequence. Single-line notes use `yaml_scalar()`, multi-line notes use YAML block scalar (`|`) with 4-space indent

5. `Store::write_ticket(&self, ticket: &Ticket) -> Result<()>`:
   - Build string starting with `---\n`
   - Write fields in order: id, title (yaml_scalar), status (as_str), type (as_str), priority, tags (write_yaml_array), assignee (null if None), estimate (null if None), deps, links, parent (null if None), created
   - Multiline optional fields (description, design, acceptance): use YAML block scalar `|` with 2-space indent per line
   - Write notes via write_yaml_notes
   - Close with `---\n`
   - Append body if present and non-empty
   - **NEVER write computed fields** (blocks, children) or body field to YAML
   - Strip null bytes from all string values before writing
   - Atomic write: write to `{id}.md.tmp`, then `fs::rename` to `{id}.md`. Both in same directory.

## Files
- `src/store.rs` — modify: add write functions to existing Store module

## Dependencies
- Depends on: vima-wmz (Store read) — extends Store with write capability
- Produces: write_ticket used by create, update, close, and all mutation commands

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Unit test: `needs_quoting("hello")` → false, `needs_quoting("true")` → true, `needs_quoting(": colon")` → true, `needs_quoting("")` → true
2. Unit test: `yaml_scalar("has \"quotes\"")` → `"has \"quotes\""`
3. **Critical round-trip test**: construct a Ticket with EVERY field populated (including multi-line description, notes with special YAML chars `: # "`, empty tags vec, assignee with spaces, estimate, parent, links, deps). Write via write_ticket, read back via read_ticket, assert all fields match. This test guards against template-writer drift.
4. Unit test: write_ticket does NOT write blocks or children to the file
5. Unit test: atomic write leaves no `.md.tmp` file on success
6. Unit test: values with null bytes have them stripped
7. `cargo build` and `cargo test` pass

title: Filter module
description:
## Context
Part of the vima implementation. Plan: `PLAN-REFINED.md`, Phase 2, Step 2.3.
WISHLIST #6: consistent filters on all list commands.

## Task
Create `src/filter.rs` with:

1. `Filter` struct:
   ```rust
   pub struct Filter {
       pub status: Option<Status>,
       pub tags: Vec<String>,           // OR: match any
       pub ticket_type: Option<TicketType>,
       pub priority_range: Option<(u8, u8)>,  // inclusive
       pub assignee: Option<String>,
       pub limit: Option<usize>,
   }
   ```

2. `Filter::matches(&self, ticket: &Ticket) -> bool`:
   - Status: if set, ticket.status must equal
   - Tags: if non-empty, ticket must have at least one matching tag (OR semantics)
   - Type: if set, ticket.ticket_type must equal
   - Priority range: if set, ticket.priority must be within [lo, hi] inclusive
   - Assignee: if set, ticket.assignee must equal Some(assignee)
   - Limit is NOT checked in matches — applied after filtering

3. `parse_priority_range(s: &str) -> Result<(u8, u8)>`:
   - `"0-2"` → (0, 2)
   - `"1"` → (1, 1)
   - Validate lo <= hi, hi <= 4
   - Return InvalidField on bad format

4. `Filter::from_args(args: &FilterArgs) -> Result<Filter>`:
   - Convert CLI FilterArgs (strings) into typed Filter
   - Status and TicketType are already typed via ValueEnum on FilterArgs
   - Parse priority string via parse_priority_range
   - Tags from Vec<String> on FilterArgs

5. `apply_filters(tickets: Vec<Ticket>, filter: &Filter) -> Vec<Ticket>`:
   - Filter, sort by (priority ASC, id ASC), apply limit
   - Return filtered vec

## Files
- `src/filter.rs` — create: Filter struct and all filtering logic
- `src/main.rs` — modify: add `mod filter;`

## Dependencies
- Depends on: vima-ii2 (Ticket data model) — references Status, TicketType, Ticket
- Produces: filtering pipeline used by list, ready, blocked, closed commands

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Unit test: matches with status filter — only matching status passes
2. Unit test: matches with multiple tags — OR semantics (any tag matches)
3. Unit test: matches with priority range "0-2" — priorities 0,1,2 pass, 3,4 fail
4. Unit test: parse_priority_range("0-2") → (0, 2), ("3") → (3, 3)
5. Unit test: parse_priority_range("3-1") → error (lo > hi)
6. Unit test: parse_priority_range("0-5") → error (hi > 4)
7. Unit test: combined filters — all conditions must be true (AND across different filter types)
8. Unit test: apply_filters sorts by priority ASC then id ASC
9. Unit test: apply_filters respects limit
10. `cargo build` and `cargo test` pass

title: Ticket data model
description:
## Context
Part of the vima implementation. Plan: `PLAN-REFINED.md`, Phase 1, Step 1.3.
The Ticket struct is the core data model — every command reads or writes it.

## Task
Create `src/ticket.rs` with:

1. `Status` enum:
   - Variants: `Open`, `InProgress`, `Closed`
   - Derive: `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize`
   - `#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]`
   - Derive `clap::ValueEnum` for CLI parsing (produces same strings as serde)
   - `as_str(&self) -> &'static str` method: `Open => "open"`, `InProgress => "in_progress"`, `Closed => "closed"`
   - `Display` impl delegating to `as_str()`

2. `TicketType` enum:
   - Variants: `Bug`, `Feature`, `Task`, `Epic`, `Chore`
   - Same derives and impls as Status
   - `as_str()`: `Bug => "bug"`, `Feature => "feature"`, `Task => "task"`, `Epic => "epic"`, `Chore => "chore"`

3. `Note` struct:
   - Fields: `timestamp: String`, `text: String`
   - Derive: `Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize`

4. `Ticket` struct with exact field order and serde attributes:
   ```rust
   pub struct Ticket {
       pub id: String,
       pub title: String,
       pub status: Status,
       #[serde(rename = "type")]
       pub ticket_type: TicketType,
       pub priority: u8,                    // 0-4
       #[serde(default)]
       pub tags: Vec<String>,
       #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
       pub assignee: Option<String>,
       #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
       pub estimate: Option<u32>,           // minutes
       #[serde(default)]
       pub deps: Vec<String>,
       #[serde(default)]
       pub links: Vec<String>,
       #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
       pub parent: Option<String>,
       pub created: String,                 // ISO 8601
       #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
       pub description: Option<String>,
       #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
       pub design: Option<String>,
       #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
       pub acceptance: Option<String>,
       #[serde(default)]
       pub notes: Vec<Note>,
       #[serde(skip)]
       pub body: Option<String>,            // markdown body, not in JSON
       #[serde(default, skip_deserializing)]
       pub blocks: Vec<String>,             // computed: reverse deps
       #[serde(default, skip_deserializing)]
       pub children: Vec<String>,           // computed: reverse parent
   }
   ```
   Derive: `Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize`

5. Update `src/cli.rs`: replace any placeholder Status/TicketType with real imports from `ticket.rs`. Ensure `CreateArgs`, `UpdateArgs`, `FilterArgs` use `Option<Status>` and `Option<TicketType>` where applicable.

## Files
- `src/ticket.rs` — create: all structs and enums
- `src/cli.rs` — modify: use real Status/TicketType enums
- `src/main.rs` — modify: add `mod ticket;`

## Dependencies
- Depends on: vima-2ea (Project scaffold) — needs Cargo.toml with serde/clap deps
- Produces: Ticket struct used by store, output, filter, and all commands

## Acceptance Criteria
1. `cargo build` succeeds
2. Unit test: `Status::InProgress.as_str()` returns `"in_progress"`
3. Unit test: `TicketType::Bug.as_str()` returns `"bug"`
4. Unit test: Serialize a Ticket to JSON → `type` field appears (not `ticket_type`), `body` is absent, `blocks`/`children` appear as empty arrays
5. Unit test: Deserialize JSON into Ticket → `blocks`/`children` are empty (skip_deserializing), optional fields missing → None
6. Unit test: serde round-trip — serialize then deserialize preserves all fields except computed/body

title: Plugin system + help command
description:
## Context
Part of the vima implementation. Plan: `PLAN-REFINED.md`, Phase 4, Step 4.3.
Plugins are executables named `vima-<cmd>` on PATH. Matches git's plugin model.

## Task
Create `src/plugin.rs` with:

1. `try_plugin(command: &str, args: &[String]) -> Option<()>`:
   - Construct plugin name: `vima-{command}`
   - Use `which::which(&plugin_name)` to find on PATH
   - If not found → return None
   - If found:
     a. Set env vars (inside unsafe block — single-threaded, about to exec):
        - `VIMA_DIR` → result of find_vima_root() (best-effort, skip if no .vima/)
        - `VIMA_TICKETS_DIR` → VIMA_DIR/tickets
        - `VIMA_BIN` → std::env::current_exe()
     b. Call `exec::Command::new(&path).args(args).exec()` — replaces process
     c. If exec fails, eprintln error and exit(1)

2. Wire `Commands::External(args)` in `src/main.rs`:
   - `args[0]` is command name, `args[1..]` are arguments
   - Call `plugin::try_plugin(cmd, &plugin_args)`
   - If returns None (no plugin found): log_error with InvalidField("unknown command: {cmd}"), exit 1

3. Wire `Commands::Help` in `src/main.rs`:
   - Print standard clap help
   - Scan PATH for executables matching `vima-*` pattern
   - For each found plugin:
     a. Try to read first 10 lines of the file
     b. Look for `# vima-plugin: <description>` comment
     c. If found, display: `  {command}    {description}`
   - If no plugins found, skip the plugins section

4. `discover_plugins() -> Vec<(String, Option<String>)>`:
   - Scan each PATH directory for files starting with `vima-`
   - Extract command name (strip `vima-` prefix)
   - Extract description from file header
   - Return vec of (command, optional_description)

## Files
- `src/plugin.rs` — create: try_plugin, discover_plugins
- `src/main.rs` — modify: wire External and Help commands, add `mod plugin;`

## Dependencies
- Depends on: vima-nyp (create command) — need a working vima binary for testing
- Produces: plugin extensibility and enhanced help command

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Integration test: create a script `vima-test-plugin` on PATH that echoes args, run `vima test-plugin arg1` → script executes with arg1
2. Integration test: `vima nonexistent-command` → exit 1, "unknown command" error
3. Integration test: `vima help` shows built-in commands
4. Integration test: with `vima-test-plugin` on PATH, `vima help` lists it
5. Unit test: discover_plugins finds `vima-*` executables
6. Verify VIMA_DIR and VIMA_BIN env vars are set for plugin
7. `cargo build` and `cargo test` pass

title: Error types + JSON stderr output
description:
## Context
Part of the vima implementation. Plan: `PLAN-REFINED.md`, Phase 1, Step 1.2.
All errors must serialize as JSON to stderr with structured data. Stdout is exclusively for data.

## Task
Create `src/error.rs` with:

1. `Error` enum with these variants (exhaustive, no wildcard matches anywhere):
   - `NotFound(String)` — ticket ID not found
   - `AmbiguousId(String, Vec<String>)` — partial match returned multiple
   - `Cycle(Vec<String>)` — dependency cycle detected
   - `InvalidBackref(String)` — batch $N reference invalid
   - `IdExists(String)` — ID collision
   - `InvalidField(String)` — validation failure
   - `NoVimaDir` — no .vima/ found
   - `IoError(std::io::Error)` — filesystem errors
   - `YamlError(String)` — parse errors

2. Implement on `Error`:
   - `code(&self) -> &'static str` — returns snake_case error code (e.g., `"not_found"`, `"ambiguous_id"`, `"cycle"`, etc.)
   - `exit_code(&self) -> i32` — returns 2 for `Cycle`, 1 for all others
   - `Display` impl with human-readable messages
   - `std::error::Error` impl

3. Implement `From` conversions:
   - `From<std::io::Error>` for `Error` (wraps in `IoError`)
   - `From<serde_json::Error>` for `Error` (wraps in `YamlError` with message)

4. Add to `output.rs` (or a new section in `error.rs`):
   - `log_error(err: &Error)` — serializes error as JSON to stderr. Format: `{"error": "<code>", "message": "<display>"}`. Add structured data for specific variants:
     - `AmbiguousId` → include `"matches": [...]`
     - `Cycle` → include `"cycle": [...]`
   - Use exhaustive match (no wildcard) so compiler catches missing variants

5. Wire into `src/main.rs`: wrap the command dispatch in a result handler that calls `log_error` and `std::process::exit(err.exit_code())` on `Err`.

6. Define `pub type Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, Error>;` in error.rs for ergonomic usage.

## Files
- `src/error.rs` — create: Error enum, impls, log_error
- `src/main.rs` — modify: add error handling around dispatch

## Dependencies
- Depends on: vima-2ea (Project scaffold + clap skeleton) — needs compilable project
- Produces: Error type and Result alias used by every subsequent bead

## Acceptance Criteria
1. `cargo build` succeeds
2. Unit test: `Error::NotFound("x".into()).code()` returns `"not_found"`
3. Unit test: `Error::Cycle(vec![]).exit_code()` returns `2`
4. Unit test: `log_error(&Error::AmbiguousId("x".into(), vec!["a".into(), "b".into()]))` writes valid JSON to stderr containing `"matches": ["a", "b"]`
5. All Error variants have code() and exit_code() coverage
6. `From<std::io::Error>` and `From<serde_json::Error>` conversions work with `?` operator

title: CI + release workflow
description:
## Context
Part of the vima implementation. Plan: `PLAN-REFINED.md`, Phase 4, Step 4.6.
GitHub Actions for continuous integration and cross-platform release builds.

## Task

### CI workflow (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`)
1. Trigger on push to main and PRs
2. Jobs:
   - `check`: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`
   - `test`: `cargo test` on ubuntu-latest
   - Matrix: test on stable Rust
3. Cache cargo registry and target directory

### Release workflow (`.github/workflows/release.yml`)
1. Trigger on tag push (`v*`)
2. Build matrix:
   - `x86_64-unknown-linux-musl` — fully static binary
   - `aarch64-unknown-linux-musl`
   - `x86_64-apple-darwin`
   - `aarch64-apple-darwin`
3. For musl targets: install musl tools, add rustup target
4. Build: `cargo build --release --target <target>`
5. Strip binaries (already in Cargo.toml profile but verify)
6. Create GitHub release with all binaries
7. Name binaries: `vima-<os>-<arch>` (e.g., `vima-linux-x86_64`, `vima-darwin-arm64`)

### Verify installation
8. Add step to CI: `cargo install --path .` succeeds

## Files
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — create: CI workflow
- `.github/workflows/release.yml` — create: release workflow

## Dependencies
- Depends on: vima-a7q (batch create) — all features complete before CI
- Depends on: vima-lm7 (pretty output) — all features complete
- Depends on: vima-iim (plugin system) — all features complete
- Depends on: vima-u70 (init --with-instructions) — all features complete
- Produces: automated CI/CD pipeline

## Acceptance Criteria
1. CI workflow YAML is valid GitHub Actions syntax
2. CI runs fmt check, clippy, and tests
3. Release workflow builds for all 4 targets
4. Release binaries are named consistently
5. `cargo install --path .` step is included in CI
6. Workflows use cargo caching for faster runs

title: Pretty output for list + show
description:
## Context
Part of the vima implementation. Plan: `PLAN-REFINED.md`, Phase 4, Steps 4.1-4.2.
Human-readable output mode via --pretty flag. JSON remains the default.

## Task
Add `--pretty` output support to `src/output.rs`:

### List pretty (table format)
1. When --pretty is set on list/ready/blocked/closed commands, render as aligned table:
   ```
   ID       P  STATUS       TYPE   TITLE
   vm-a3x9  1  open         bug    Fix auth middleware
   vm-b7k2  2  in_progress  task   Add rate limiter
   ```
2. Column widths: compute max width for each column from data, with reasonable minimums
3. Header row with column names
4. Truncate title if terminal width is known (optional — can use a fixed max)

### Show pretty (formatted sections)
1. When --pretty is set on show command, render formatted ticket:
   ```
   vm-a3x9 — Fix auth middleware
   Status: open  Type: bug  Priority: 1
   Tags: backend, auth
   Assignee: alice  Estimate: 30m
   Created: 2026-04-02T12:00:00Z

   Description:
     The auth middleware stores session tokens...

   Deps: vm-b7k2
   Blocks: vm-c4m1
   ```
2. Use `colored` crate for colors: status gets color (open=green, in_progress=yellow, closed=gray), priority 0-1 get red
3. Use `colored::control::set_override(true)` when --pretty is set, `set_override(false)` otherwise (prevents color codes in JSON output)

### Dep tree pretty
1. When --pretty on dep tree, render ASCII tree:
   ```
   vm-a3x9 Fix auth middleware (open)
   ├── vm-b7k2 Add rate limiter (in_progress)
   │   └── vm-c4m1 Update docs (open)
   └── vm-d5n2 Fix tests (closed)
   ```
2. Tree connectors: `├── `, `└── `, `│   `, `    `

### Wiring --pretty into commands
3. Wire `--pretty` flag (from `Cli.pretty`) into the output path of list, ready, blocked, closed, show, and dep tree commands. When `--pretty` is true, call the pretty formatters instead of JSON output. When false, existing JSON output is unchanged.

## Files
- `src/output.rs` — modify: add pretty output functions (pretty_list, pretty_show, pretty_tree)
- `src/main.rs` — modify: pass `cli.pretty` to output functions and branch on it

## Dependencies
- Depends on: vima-08i (list + closed) — need list commands to test pretty list output
- Depends on: vima-e64 (show command) — need show command to test pretty show output
- Depends on: vima-dda (dep tree command) — need dep tree command to test pretty tree output
- Produces: human-readable output mode for all display commands

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Integration test: `vima list --pretty` outputs table with header row and aligned columns
2. Integration test: `vima show <id> --pretty` outputs formatted sections
3. Integration test: without --pretty, output is still valid JSON (no color codes leak)
4. Integration test: `vima dep tree <id> --pretty` outputs ASCII tree with connectors
5. Integration test: empty list with --pretty shows just the header (or "No tickets found")
6. `cargo build` and `cargo test` pass

title: create command (single mode)
description:
## Context
Part of the vima implementation. Plan: `PLAN-REFINED.md`, Phase 1, Step 1.7 (create portion).
The most complex single command. Handles ID generation, relationship wiring, and validation.

## Task
Wire the `create` command (single mode only — batch is a separate bead) in `src/main.rs`:

1. Require `title` — return `Error::InvalidField("title is required")` if missing
2. Generate ID:
   - If `--id` provided: validate via `validate_id()`, check no collision in `.vima/tickets/`
   - Else: call `generate_id(prefix, tickets_dir)` which uses `{prefix}-{nanoid(4, alphanumeric)}` with retry
3. Validate priority 0-4 if provided; return `Error::InvalidField("priority must be 0-4")` otherwise
4. Build Ticket struct with defaults: status=Open, priority=2, type=Task, created=now (ISO 8601 via jiff), assignee=None
5. Parse `--tags` as comma-separated into Vec<String>
6. Handle `--dep`: resolve each dep ID via `store.resolve_id(dep, exact)`, verify target exists, store in ticket.deps. Cycle detection for --dep is NOT wired in this bead — it's added when the dep add bead (vima-f9c) implements `would_create_cycle` in deps.rs and wires it back into create.
7. Handle `--parent`: resolve parent ID, verify exists, store in ticket.parent
8. Write ticket to `.vima/tickets/{id}.md` via `store.write_ticket()`
9. Handle `--blocks`: for each target, load target ticket, add this ticket's ID to target's deps, save target. This happens AFTER step 8.
10. Stderr: `Created {id}`
11. Stdout: full ticket JSON via `output_one(&ticket, &pluck)`. For a newly created ticket, `blocks` and `children` are always empty arrays (no other ticket references it yet), so calling `compute_reverse_fields` is unnecessary — just output the ticket directly after writing.

## Files
- `src/main.rs` — modify: implement create command handler

## Dependencies
- Depends on: vima-gz3 (YAML writer) — needs write_ticket
- Depends on: vima-ypm (Output layer) — needs output_one
- Depends on: vima-59k (init command) — init must work before create (tests need it); also provides Store::resolve_id
- Produces: ticket creation used by all subsequent command tests, batch create builds on this

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Integration test: `vima create "Fix auth" -t bug -p 1` → stdout is valid JSON with title="Fix auth", type="bug", priority=1, status="open"
2. Integration test: `vima create "Test" --id my-id-01` → ticket file is `my-id-01.md`, JSON has id="my-id-01"
3. Integration test: `vima create` (no title) → exit 1, stderr JSON with error="invalid_field"
4. Integration test: `vima create "A" --id ../traversal` → exit 1, invalid characters error
5. Integration test: `vima create "A" -p 5` → exit 1, priority must be 0-4
6. Integration test: create with `--tags "backend,auth"` → tags=["backend","auth"] in JSON
7. Integration test: create with `--dep <existing-id>` → deps populated in output
8. Integration test: create with `--blocks <existing-id>` → target ticket's deps updated
9. Integration test: stderr contains "Created {id}"
10. `cargo build` and `cargo test` pass

title: update command
description:
## Context
Part of the vima implementation. Plan: `PLAN-REFINED.md`, Phase 2, Step 2.2 (update portion).
WISHLIST #2: `tk update` for any field. The primary mutation command.

## Task
Wire the `update` command in `src/main.rs`:

1. Resolve ticket ID via store.resolve_id
2. Load ticket via store.read_ticket
3. For each provided flag, REPLACE the field value (not merge/append):
   - `--title` → ticket.title
   - `--description` → ticket.description (Some(val), or None if empty string)
   - `--design` → ticket.design (same empty-string-clears pattern)
   - `--acceptance` → ticket.acceptance
   - `--priority` → validate 0-4, return InvalidField if out of range
   - `--tags` → parse comma-separated, replace entire tags vec
   - `--assignee` → Some(val), or None if empty string `""`
   - `--estimate` → ticket.estimate
   - `--status` → ticket.status (clap validates enum via ValueEnum)
   - `--type` → ticket.ticket_type
4. Write ticket back via store.write_ticket
5. Reload with store.load_and_compute(id) for computed fields
6. Stdout: full updated ticket JSON via output_one
7. Stderr: `Updated {id}`

Key detail: `--assignee ""` sets assignee to None. `--description ""` sets description to None. This is the ONLY way to clear optional fields.

## Files
- `src/main.rs` — modify: implement update command handler

## Dependencies
- Depends on: vima-nyp (create command) — need tickets to update (for tests)
- Depends on: vima-uky (reverse field computation) — needs load_and_compute for output
- Produces: update command; start/close/reopen are thin wrappers around similar logic

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Integration test: create ticket, `vima update <id> --title "New title"` → title changed in output
2. Integration test: `vima update <id> --priority 0` → priority=0 in output
3. Integration test: `vima update <id> --priority 5` → exit 1, invalid_field
4. Integration test: `vima update <id> --tags "a,b,c"` → tags=["a","b","c"]
5. Integration test: `vima update <id> --assignee "alice"` then `--assignee ""` → assignee=null
6. Integration test: `vima update <id> --description ""` → description absent from JSON
7. Integration test: `vima update <id> --status in_progress` → status="in_progress"
8. stderr contains "Updated {id}"
9. `cargo build` and `cargo test` pass

title: init --with-instructions (CLAUDE.md)
description:
## Context
Part of the vima implementation. Plan: `PLAN-REFINED.md`, Phase 4, Step 4.4.
Optional CLAUDE.md generation with vima usage instructions for AI agents.

## Task
Extend the init command in `src/main.rs`:

1. When `--with-instructions` flag is set:
   a. Check if `CLAUDE.md` already exists in CWD
   b. If exists → return error: `Error::InvalidField("CLAUDE.md already exists — merge manually")`
   c. If not exists → write a starter CLAUDE.md with vima usage instructions

2. CLAUDE.md content should include:
   - Brief description of vima as the project's ticket tracker
   - Common commands: create, list, ready, show, update, close, start
   - JSON output format explanation
   - --pluck and --count usage
   - Batch create with back-references example
   - dep add / is-ready workflow
   - Note about --exact mode for automation

3. The CLAUDE.md should be concise and practical — it's instructions for an AI agent, not documentation for humans.

## Files
- `src/main.rs` — modify: add --with-instructions handling to init command

## Dependencies
- Depends on: vima-59k (init command) — extends existing init implementation
- Produces: optional agent instruction file

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Integration test: `vima init --with-instructions` creates CLAUDE.md with vima usage content
2. Integration test: `vima init --with-instructions` when CLAUDE.md exists → error, file unchanged
3. Integration test: `vima init` (without flag) does NOT create CLAUDE.md
4. Integration test: CLAUDE.md content mentions key commands (create, list, ready, close)
5. `cargo build` and `cargo test` pass

title: Reverse field computation (blocks/children)
description:
## Context
Part of the vima implementation. Plan: `PLAN-REFINED.md`, Phase 2, Step 2.1.
Computed reverse fields (blocks, children) appear in every JSON output. They are NEVER stored on disk.

## Task
Create `src/deps.rs` with:

1. `compute_reverse_fields(tickets: &mut [Ticket])`:
   - Build `blocks_map: HashMap<String, Vec<String>>` — for each ticket, for each dep, map dep → ticket.id
   - Build `children_map: HashMap<String, Vec<String>>` — for each ticket with parent, map parent → ticket.id
   - Apply maps to tickets: `t.blocks = blocks_map.remove(&t.id).unwrap_or_default()`
   - Same for children

2. Add `Store::load_and_compute(&self, id: &str) -> Result<Ticket>`:
   - Call `self.read_all()`
   - Call `compute_reverse_fields()` on the vec
   - Find and return the ticket matching `id`
   - This is the reload-after-write helper used by all mutating commands

## Files
- `src/deps.rs` — create: compute_reverse_fields, will also hold cycle detection later
- `src/store.rs` — modify: add load_and_compute method
- `src/main.rs` — modify: add `mod deps;`

## Dependencies
- Depends on: vima-wmz (Store read) — needs read_all to load all tickets
- Produces: reverse field computation used by show, list, ready, blocked, and all mutation command outputs

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Unit test: 3 tickets where A deps=[B], B deps=[C] → B.blocks=[A], C.blocks=[B], A.blocks=[]
2. Unit test: ticket with parent=X → X.children contains ticket.id
3. Unit test: empty deps/parent → empty blocks/children
4. Unit test: load_and_compute returns ticket with populated blocks/children
5. `cargo build` and `cargo test` pass

title: start + close + reopen commands
description:
## Context
Part of the vima implementation. Plan: `PLAN-REFINED.md`, Phase 2, Step 2.2.
WISHLIST #5: `tk close --reason` for atomic close-with-note. start and reopen are thin status transitions.

## Task
Wire three commands in `src/main.rs`:

### start
1. Resolve ID, load ticket
2. If already in_progress → no-op, return current state
3. Set status = InProgress
4. Write, reload with load_and_compute, output_one
5. Stderr: `Started {id}`

### close
1. Accept multiple IDs (Vec<String> in CloseArgs)
2. For each ID:
   a. Resolve and load ticket
   b. If already closed → no-op (skip write, no duplicate note)
   c. Set status = Closed
   d. If `--reason` provided, append Note { timestamp: now(), text: reason }
   e. Write ticket
3. Stdout: JSON array of closed tickets (always array, even for single ID)
4. Stderr: `Closed {id}` for each

### reopen
1. Resolve ID, load ticket
2. If already open → no-op
3. Set status = Open (even from in_progress — valid use case)
4. Write, reload, output_one
5. Stderr: `Reopened {id}`

## Files
- `src/main.rs` — modify: implement start, close, reopen command handlers

## Dependencies
- Depends on: vima-pg1 (update command) — shares the load-mutate-write-reload pattern
- Produces: close command needed by ready/blocked (checks dep status); start/reopen for workflow

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Integration test: `vima start <id>` → status="in_progress"
2. Integration test: `vima start <id>` on in_progress ticket → no error, same state
3. Integration test: `vima close <id>` → status="closed"
4. Integration test: `vima close <id> --reason "Done"` → status="closed", notes contains reason with timestamp
5. Integration test: `vima close <id>` on already-closed → no error, no duplicate note
6. Integration test: `vima close <id1> <id2>` → JSON array with both tickets
7. Integration test: `vima reopen <id>` → status="open"
8. Integration test: `vima reopen <id>` on open ticket → no error
9. `cargo build` and `cargo test` pass

title: dep cycle — global cycle detection
description:
## Context
Part of the vima implementation. Plan: `PLAN-REFINED.md`, Phase 2, Step 2.8.
Global cycle detection across all open tickets — a diagnostic tool, not a guard (that's would_create_cycle in dep add).

## Task
Add `dep cycle` subcommand:

1. In `src/deps.rs`, add `detect_all_cycles(tickets: &[Ticket]) -> Vec<Vec<String>>`:
   - Filter to non-closed tickets only
   - Build adjacency list from deps
   - Run DFS with 3-color marking: white=unvisited, gray=in-progress, black=done
   - When gray node encountered during DFS: extract cycle from current path
   - Normalize each cycle: rotate so lexicographically smallest ID is first (for dedup)
   - Deduplicate cycles (same cycle can be found from different starting nodes)
   - Return Vec of cycles, each cycle is Vec<String> of IDs

2. Wire `DepCommands::Cycle` in `src/main.rs`:
   - Load all tickets
   - Call detect_all_cycles
   - Stdout: `{"cycles": [[...], [...]]}` — JSON object with cycles array
   - Exit code: 0 if no cycles, 2 if cycles detected

## Files
- `src/deps.rs` — modify: add detect_all_cycles with 3-color DFS
- `src/main.rs` — modify: wire dep cycle command

## Dependencies
- Depends on: vima-f9c (dep add + undep) — deps.rs exists with would_create_cycle; need dep add for test setup
- Produces: diagnostic tool for finding cycles in existing data

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Integration test: create A→B→C (no cycle), `vima dep cycle` → exit 0, `{"cycles": []}`
2. Integration test: manually create A→B, B→A cycle (by editing files or using two dep adds with --id to avoid guard), `vima dep cycle` → exit 2, cycles contains [A, B]
3. Unit test: detect_all_cycles with known 3-node cycle → returns normalized cycle
4. Unit test: detect_all_cycles skips closed tickets
5. Unit test: cycle normalization — [C, A, B] → [A, B, C]
6. Unit test: duplicate cycles are removed
7. `cargo build` and `cargo test` pass

title: Store: directory discovery + read operations
description:
## Context
Part of the vima implementation. Plan: `PLAN-REFINED.md`, Phase 1, Step 1.5 (read half).
The Store is the central abstraction for ticket I/O. This bead covers discovery and reading; writing is in the next bead.

## Task
Create `src/store.rs` with:

1. `find_vima_root() -> Result<PathBuf>`:
   - Check `VIMA_DIR` env var first. If set, verify it's a directory, canonicalize, return. If not a dir, return `Error::InvalidField("VIMA_DIR points to non-existent directory")`
   - Walk up from CWD: check for `.vima/` directory at each level
   - If root reached without finding `.vima/`, return `Error::NoVimaDir`

2. `Store` struct:
   ```rust
   pub struct Store {
       root: PathBuf,       // .vima/
       tickets: PathBuf,    // .vima/tickets/
   }
   ```

3. `Store::open() -> Result<Self>`:
   - Call `find_vima_root()` to get root
   - Set `tickets = root.join("tickets")`
   - If tickets dir doesn't exist, create it (`fs::create_dir`)
   - Return `Store { root, tickets }`

4. `Store::read_ticket(&self, id: &str) -> Result<Ticket>`:
   - Read file at `self.tickets.join(format!("{}.md", id))`
   - Use `gray_matter` to split frontmatter from body
   - Deserialize frontmatter YAML into `Ticket` via serde
   - Store markdown body in `ticket.body` if non-empty
   - Return IoError/YamlError on failure

5. `Store::read_all(&self) -> Result<Vec<Ticket>>`:
   - `fs::read_dir` on `self.tickets` (flat, no recursion)
   - Filter to `.md` extension, exclude `.md.tmp`
   - Skip symlinks via `entry.file_type()?.is_symlink()`
   - For each file: attempt `read_ticket`. On parse failure, log warning to stderr and skip (don't abort)
   - Return vec of successfully parsed tickets

6. `Store::tickets_dir(&self) -> &Path` — getter for tickets path
7. `Store::root(&self) -> &Path` — getter for root path

**NOTE:** Do NOT add `Store::resolve_id` in this bead — `id.rs` does not exist yet as a dependency. The `resolve_id` convenience method is added in the init command bead (vima-59k) which has both `store.rs` and `id.rs` available.

## Files
- `src/store.rs` — create: Store struct, find_vima_root, read operations
- `src/main.rs` — modify: add `mod store;`

## Dependencies
- Depends on: vima-ksx (Error types) — uses Error::NoVimaDir, IoError, YamlError, InvalidField
- Depends on: vima-ii2 (Ticket data model) — deserializes into Ticket struct
- Produces: Store struct used by every command. Read operations needed before write (next bead).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Unit test: `find_vima_root()` finds `.vima/` in a temp dir
2. Unit test: `find_vima_root()` walks up to parent directory
3. Unit test: `find_vima_root()` returns NoVimaDir when none exists
4. Unit test: `find_vima_root()` respects VIMA_DIR env var
5. Unit test: `read_ticket` parses a valid ticket file with all fields
6. Unit test: `read_ticket` preserves markdown body
7. Unit test: `read_all` skips unparseable files without aborting
8. Unit test: `read_all` excludes `.md.tmp` files
9. `cargo build` and `cargo test` pass

title: Output layer: JSON + pluck + count
description:
## Context
Part of the vima implementation. Plan: `PLAN-REFINED.md`, Phase 1, Step 1.6.
JSON output is the default for every command. --pluck and --count eliminate most jq usage.

## Task
Create `src/output.rs` with:

1. `output_one(ticket: &Ticket, pluck: &Option<String>) -> Result<()>`:
   - Serialize ticket to serde_json::Value
   - If pluck: extract fields via pluck_value(), print JSON
   - Else: print full JSON object
   - Propagate serialization errors (don't unwrap)

2. `output_many(tickets: &[Ticket], pluck: &Option<String>, count: bool) -> Result<()>`:
   - If count: print `tickets.len()` as raw integer (no JSON wrapper), ignore pluck, return
   - Serialize all tickets to Vec<serde_json::Value>, propagate errors
   - If pluck: call output_plucked
   - Else: print JSON array

3. `pluck_value(value: &serde_json::Value, fields: &str) -> serde_json::Value`:
   - Split fields by comma, trim each
   - Single field → return bare value (or Null if missing)
   - Multiple fields → return object with only those keys

4. `output_plucked(values: &[serde_json::Value], fields: &str)`:
   - Map each value through pluck_value, collect into array, print

5. `log(msg: &str)`:
   - `eprintln!("{}", msg)` — simple stderr logging for confirmations

## Files
- `src/output.rs` — create: all output functions
- `src/main.rs` — modify: add `mod output;`

## Dependencies
- Depends on: vima-ii2 (Ticket data model) — serializes Ticket structs
- Produces: output functions used by every command handler

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Unit test: `pluck_value` with single field returns bare value
2. Unit test: `pluck_value` with multiple fields returns object with only those keys
3. Unit test: `pluck_value` with missing field returns Null
4. Unit test: `output_many` with count=true prints integer only
5. Unit test: `output_one` without pluck prints full JSON object
6. `cargo build` and `cargo test` pass
